title: Append-only worker attempt ledger
goal: Separate immutable worker-attempt identity from retry budget and execution epoch so resume and retry can never overwrite evidence, branches, artifacts, or activity records.
acceptance: Use behavioral TDD: first add resume/retry tests that expose deterministic attempt-N reuse and evidence overwrite, then implement a monotonic append-only model without weakening those tests.

A persisted WorkerAttemptLedger allocates a unique monotonic attempt identity per run/slice launch. Retry-budget ordinal, repair ordinal, and execution/resume epoch are separate typed fields rather than being overloaded into one attempt number.

Attempt allocation and initial running evidence are transactional and happen before launch; an allocated-but-not-launched attempt remains explicit evidence and is reconciled rather than silently reused.
Attempt artifact directories/files, worker branches, output spill paths, question tokens, and status records use the immutable attempt identity and can never overwrite prior attempts after resume.
Resume increments/records an execution epoch and schedules only unfinished work while preserving every prior worker result, raw output, activity observation, branch reference, and failure cause.
Read models and reports expose stable attempt history while preserving compatibility for existing runs that only contain legacy attempt ordinals.
Direct Pi worker cancellation terminates the supervised process group/tree with configured grace and escalation, matching shell supervision rather than killing only the immediate child PID.
Tests cover retry, repair, interrupted launch, daemon restart/resume, cancellation with a grandchild process, and two resumes without duplicate identities or evidence loss.

Compatibility revision grant: CA-03 may update the daemon, IPC, workflow-event, CLI attention, cockpit, and khazad-worker extension boundaries needed to carry immutable launch identity end to end. The launch_id wire field is additive and optional to decode. Persisted legacy ordinal-only runs and questions remain readable, and requests that omit launch_id continue only through a previously issued legacy run-scoped token; new ledger-backed launches issue launch-scoped credentials and require launch_id for authorization.
must_ask_if: A migration would discard or rewrite existing attempt evidence rather than reading it compatibly.
Worker-wire JSON would be expanded with daemon-owned attempt enrichment instead of using an explicit conversion boundary.
Process-group supervision would break supported non-Unix behavior without a documented fallback.
The implementation would reset retry budgets or silently relaunch completed slices.
